title: Rewrite Sunday YT scraper: RSS-based multi-video catch + correct titles + DB cleanup
description: Two bugs found in skills/wiki-ingest/check-claude-code-videos.py: (1) only most recent video per channel ingested per Sunday run — multi-publish weeks lose videos; (2) title regex matches a YouTube UI element ('Combinaciones de teclas') instead of real video titles. Fix: switch get_latest_video() to YouTube RSS feed (https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=<ID>), iterate entries, ingest all newer than last_video_id, ingest oldest-first so state ends on newest. RSS provides clean titles, fixes bug 2 automatically. Also clean up 9 DB rows (ids 270-273 from 2026-05-10 and 289-293 from 2026-05-15) plus their .md files in claude-code-wiki/raw/transcripts/, then reset state file so next Sunday picks up real titles.
close_reason: Rewrote check-claude-code-videos.py to use YouTube RSS feeds (xml.etree.ElementTree, no new deps) — iterates entries newest-first, stops at last seen video_id, ingests oldest-first so state ends on newest. URL-dedup in add_to_database now returns True/False so we skip wiki export when DB insert is a duplicate. Discovered the original channel_ids in the config were wrong/stale (all returned 404 from RSS) — resolved real channel_ids from each @handle and updated. Cleaned up 9 bogus DB rows (270-273 from 2026-05-10, 289-293 from 2026-05-15), 500 segment rows, 10 wiki .md files. Reset state file. Re-ran with new code: 23 real videos ingested across all 5 channels with correct titles ('Make the PERFECT Videos with Claude Code', 'Claude Code Has Evolved', etc.).

title: Fix daily AI news cron failing since May 3 (Dropbox+venv deadlock)
description: The 9 AM com.jarvis.news-aggregator launchd job has been failing daily since 2026-05-03. Last successful digest: 2026-05-02. Root cause: Python venv lives inside Dropbox CloudStorage; macOS launchd hits 'OSError: [Errno 11] Resource deadlock avoided' loading urllib3 from the venv, and 'Operation not permitted' sourcing email-config.sh from the same Dropbox path. Fix: recreate venv at ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/venv (local-only), move email-config.sh to local path, update /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh to use the new paths.
close_reason: Recreated venv at ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/venv, moved email-config.sh to ~/.local/share/jarvis/news-aggregator/email-config.sh (mode 0600), updated /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-news-daily.sh to point at new paths. Tested end-to-end: digest for 2026-05-15 generated (42 articles) and email sent successfully. launchd job reloaded.

title: Fix weekly arxiv-research cron — launchd can't exec Dropbox-hosted wrapper
description: com.jarvis.arxiv-research launchd job fails every run with: /bin/bash: /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/arxiv-research-aggregator/weekly-arxiv.sh: Operation not permitted. Same root cause as jarvis-d18 — macOS launchd can't execute files inside Dropbox CloudStorage. Last failure 2026-05-10. Fix: relocate weekly-arxiv.sh to /Users/terrybyrd/bin/ (or another local path), update the plist's ProgramArguments to point at the new location. Verify any venv it uses is also local.
close_reason: Created /Users/terrybyrd/bin/jarvis-arxiv-weekly.sh (local), set PYTHON=/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 with absolute SCRIPT_DIR pointing to Dropbox source. Updated plist ProgramArguments to invoke the local wrapper directly. Reloaded launchd. Manual test ran cleanly (only a transient HTTP 429 from arXiv API rate-limiting, unrelated to the fix).

title: Fix weekly academic-research cron — launchd can't exec Dropbox-hosted wrapper
description: com.jarvis.academic-research launchd job fails every run with: /bin/bash: /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/academic-research-aggregator/weekly-research.sh: Operation not permitted. Same root cause as jarvis-d18 — macOS launchd can't execute files inside Dropbox CloudStorage. Last failure 2026-05-10. Fix: relocate weekly-research.sh to /Users/terrybyrd/bin/ (or another local path), update the plist's ProgramArguments to point at the new location. Verify any venv it uses is also local.
notes: Wrapper script /Users/terrybyrd/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/jarvis/skills/academic-research-aggregator/weekly-research.sh contains 'read -p "Press Enter when you're ready to continue..."' and launches Chrome interactively expecting Auburn Library login. This was never going to run from launchd — it's fundamentally an interactive script. Also cd's into /Users/terrybyrd/Dropbox/jarvis/repos/VIDEO-GENERATOR_FOUNDATIONS (a separate legacy non-CloudStorage Dropbox path). DECISION NEEDED: (a) unload launchd job and treat as manual-only, (b) split into non-interactive variant that assumes Chrome+session is already running, or (c) rewrite to use a non-Auburn data source. Until then, the cron is a no-op.
close_reason: Per user 2026-05-15: dispensing with Auburn EBSCO source — new articles are rare enough that manual checking is faster than maintaining the interactive Chrome+Auburn-login pipeline. May revisit with an API-based source (Semantic Scholar, etc.) later. Unloaded launchd job and renamed plist to com.jarvis.academic-research.plist.disabled so it won't auto-load on next login. Source scripts in skills/academic-research-aggregator/ left in place for future reference.
